task_completion.description: Navigate to the WordPress 'Add New Page' screen.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The WordPress admin dashboard is visible with the page editor open to create a new page.

task_completion.description: Enter the page title 'Terms of Service'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The title field in the WordPress page editor contains exactly 'Terms of Service'.

task_completion.description: Enter the required terms of service content.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The WordPress block editor contains the text: 'By placing an order, you agree to our standard terms of service regarding shipping, returns, and privacy.'
A 'Page published' notification or the 'Update' button (indicating it has been published) is visible in the WordPress editor.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the WooCommerce Settings page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The WooCommerce Settings page is open, typically on the 'Advanced' tab where page setups are configured.

task_completion.description: Select 'Terms of Service' as the WooCommerce Terms and conditions page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the WooCommerce settings (Advanced tab), the 'Terms and conditions' dropdown or selection field shows 'Terms of Service' selected.
A 'Your settings have been saved.' success message is visible at the top of the WooCommerce Settings page.

integrity.description: The agent used the WordPress web interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the WordPress admin dashboard in a web browser, rather than terminal commands (like WP-CLI) or direct database manipulation.

integrity.description: The page was created in the actual WordPress editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text 'Terms of Service' and the content are seen inside the standard WordPress Gutenberg or Classic editor, not a separate text editor.

integrity.description: WooCommerce settings were updated via the standard settings page.
integrity.visual_evidence: The WooCommerce settings page is visible with the dropdown interacted with, rather than a script or API tool being used to set 'woocommerce_terms_page_id'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected Page Title: 'Terms of Service'. Expected Content: 'By placing an order, you agree to our standard terms of service regarding shipping, returns, and privacy.' The WooCommerce setting to update is visually represented as the 'Terms and conditions' page dropdown, typically located under WooCommerce > Settings > Advanced. The underlying database key is 'woocommerce_terms_page_id'.
